events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse image: Evacuation decay test pump isolated. Microns @ isolation t=0 = 359. Microns @ t=10 min = 4563. So pressure rose from 359 microns to 4563 microns in 10 min. That's huge decay, indicates leak or outgassing? In refrigeration evacuation, standing decay test: isolate pump, watch micron gauge. If rises above 1000-2000 microns or doesn't hold, fail. Rise to 4563 microns in 10 min means system has significant leak/moisture/outgassing. Since cores removed, far port gauge. Pump isolated. The decay indicates leak or inadequate evacuation (moisture/contaminants). Most likely fault: leak in brazing / system not leak-free. Disposition fail. Risk high? Leak means refrigerant loss, environmental, system performance. Component condition installed-defective, non-compliant. Need output JSON with fields. value = 4563 (10-minute reading). percent_complete maybe 80? tested/inspected stage. confidence high. event_time 10.0? rate maybe (4563-359)/10 = 420.4 microns/min. order empty. workable empty. findings: excessive pressure rise during standing decay test; system fails to hold vacuum; likely leak or contamination. actions: leak test system, repair leaks, replace filter drier, re-evacuate, re-test decay. rationale: 10-min rise from 359 to 4563 microns far exceeds acceptable decay (typically <500-1000 microns or no significant rise), indicating leak or outgassing; fail. references: EPA 608 (leak repair requirements), mfr specs. Need ensure JSON valid.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 4563,

  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 10.0,
  "rate": 420.4,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Micron gauge rose from 359 microns to 4563 microns during a 10-minute standing decay test",
    "System fails to hold vacuum, indicating a significant leak and/or excessive outgassing/contamination",
    "Evacuation/brazing integrity does not meet acceptable holding/decay criteria"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform electronic/ultrasonic leak detection or nitrogen pressure test to locate leaks",
    "Repair defective brazed joints or leaking components",
    "Replace filter-drier and remove cores during evacuation",
    "Re-evacuate to manufacturer-specified microns and repeat standing decay test until it holds"
  ],
  "rationale": "A rise from 359 to 4563 microns in 10 minutes is far beyond an acceptable standing decay; the system cannot hold vacuum, so the evacuation/brazing work is defective and must be corrected before charging.",
  "references": ["EPA 608", "mfr specs"]
}
